topStories.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has expressed concerns over US rapper Kanye West's upcoming performance in the country. This comes after the 48-year-old rapper has stoked several controversies in the past over his antisemitic remarks

topStories.title: Uttarakhand man flags adverse CIBIL score for years despite no loan or default; SC rescues him—here's what happened
topStories.description: The Supreme Court provided relief to Rajendra Singh Panwar, whose credit score was inaccurately negative due to confusion with others sharing his name. Banks clarified there were no defaults, resulting in the correction of his CIBIL score, allowing him better access to financial services.

topStories.title: US Rescues Missing Airman as Iran Strikes Persian Gulf States
topStories.description: US forces rescued an airman from Iran after his fighter jet was shot down, while the Islamic Republic’s continued attacks sparked fires that damaged Kuwait’s oil headquarters and shut down an Emirati petrochemicals plant.

topStories.title: Unlike BJP, Congress works to unify people and spread love: Rahul Gandhi
topStories.description: Rahul Gandhi accuses BJP and CM Sarma of corruption and spreading hatred, pledges Congress will unify Assam and deliver development.

topStories.title: Yankees legend Reggie Jackson criticizes Pete Hegseth's leadership amid Iran conflict
topStories.description: Baseball Hall of Famer Reggie Jackson slammed Pete Hegseth on X over shaking up Army leadership during the ongoing military operation in Iran.

topStories.title: Trump calls rescue of downed Air Force pilot an 'Easter Miracle'
topStories.description: President Donald Trump praised the rescue of a downed U.S. airman in Iran as an “Easter Miracle," calling the mission one of the most daring operations in American military history.

dailyNews.title: Middle East Conflict: US destroys two of its warplanes while rescuing pilot, Trump hails operation; Iran mocks
dailyNews.description: US President Donald Trump on Sunday (local time) announced that an Air Force officer was rescued in an intense and daring nighttime operation. However, he did not mention that Washington had to destroy two of its warplanes to conduct the rescue operation. Iran has mocked Washington over the damage.

dailyNews.title: Trump issues stark warning to Iran: Reopen Strait of Hormuz or face US strikes on power plants and bridges on Tuesday
dailyNews.description: In a post on Truth Social, Trump said: “Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day, all wrapped up in one, in Iran… Open the Strait… or you’ll be living in Hell.”

dailyNews.title: Gunfire reported near Lafayette Park across from White House; US Secret Service launches probe
dailyNews.description: The US Secret Service said on Sunday (local time) that it is investigating reports of gunfire overnight near Lafayette Park, located just across the street from the White House, AP reported.

dailyNews.title: 'Open The F***** Strait': Trump Vows To Obliterate Iran's Power Plants, Bridges On Tuesday
dailyNews.description: "Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day, all wrapped up in one, in Iran. There will be nothing like it," Trump said.

dailyNews.title: Basketball legend Candace Parker takes aim at Geno Auriemma after Dawn Staley confrontation
dailyNews.description: Former WNBA star Candace Parker had no empathy for Geno Auriemma after his heated postgame confrontation with Dawn Staley at the women's Final Four on Friday night.

dailyNews.title: Democrats face backlash for leaving Biden out of Easter ‘better times’ post
dailyNews.description: The Democratic Party shared an Easter post referencing “better times at the White House" without mentioning President Joe Biden, drawing widespread reaction.

dailyNews.title: Donnie Wahlberg and Jenny McCarthy's surprising faith date night rituals after almost 12 years of marriage
dailyNews.description: Donnie Wahlberg says date nights with Jenny McCarthy now mean watching "The Chosen" at home, skipping big plans for takeout and their faith-based show.

dailyNews.title: 5 key factors may predict stroke risk years after first event, study finds
dailyNews.description: New research reveals five factors that may predict another stroke after a mini stroke, including hypertension, smoking and three specific stroke subtypes.

dailyNews.title: Trump vows US will strike Iran’s power plants, bridges if Strait of Hormuz is not reopened
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump issues a profanity-laced deadline to Iran, threatening to strike power plants and bridges Tuesday if the Strait of Hormuz isn't reopened.

dailyNews.title: Surging UK Green Party pushes church-state split, critics warn of break from Britain’s Christian roots
dailyNews.description: Critics slam the Green Party's vow to separate the Church of England from the state, calling it a direct assault on the nation's Christian heritage.

dailyNews.title: Yankees' Giancarlo Stanton steals first base since 2020 in team's win over Marlins
dailyNews.description: Giancarlo Stanton stole his first base since 2020 during the Yankees' 9-7 comeback win over the Marlins, saying he had to take what they gave him.

dailyNews.title: Celebrity chef reveals unexpected Easter dish guests doubt at first, then can't stop eating
dailyNews.description: Jason Smith, a Food Network chef, shares his must-have Easter dishes, including sautéed spring peas and a Southern cornbread salad that he says steals the show.

dailyNews.title: 'Duck Dynasty' star Sadie Robertson Huff offers a glimpse into her family’s Easter, from church to egg hunts
dailyNews.description: Sadie Robertson Huff reveals how her family blends faith traditions with fun on Easter while helping her kids understand the holiday's true meaning.

dailyNews.title: Art heist targeting million-dollar masterpieces exposes blind spots in museum defenses, expert warns
dailyNews.description: Former FBI agent Geoff Kelly says museum art thefts are more common than people think, warning that open, welcoming spaces also create security vulnerabilities.

dailyNews.title: Two secret Christians found each other in an arranged marriage miracle
dailyNews.description: In an excerpt from "The DNA of a Doctor," a couple in an arranged marriage in India discovers they both secretly share the same Christian faith.

dailyNews.title: UConn's Dan Hurley hears boos after Final Four win over Illinois
dailyNews.description: Dan Hurley and UConn advanced to the national championship after beating Illinois, but the crowd booed the coach during his postgame interview.

topStories.description: An Air Force officer of a F-15E Strike Eagle shot down on Friday by Iran spent a day in hostile territory with little more than a pistol for protection.

topStories.title: What We Know About the F-15E Strike Eagle Shot Down by Iran
topStories.description: An airman was rescued on Saturday night, President Trump said. The rescue came after a risky two-day operation deep inside Iran.

topStories.description: The architect of President Trump’s mass deportation campaign wants “a moratorium on immigration from third world countries until we can heal ourselves as a nation.” The chaos in Minneapolis has not pushed him off that course.

dailyNews.description: The Trump administration had said it would collect data from colleges to ensure compliance with a Supreme Court ruling ending affirmative action in admissions.

dailyNews.title: Secret Service investigating overnight gunfire near White House
dailyNews.description: No injuries were reported and a suspect was not located following a search of the area, the Secret Service said.

dailyNews.title: Trump says rescued U.S. airman was "seriously wounded," vows to hit Iran's power plants
dailyNews.description: As the Iran war passes the five-week mark, President Trump hailed the rescue of a U.S. airman who was missing for days inside Iran — and threatened to hit power plants if Iran doesn't let the Strait of Hormuz open.

dailyNews.title: U.S. drawn deeper into war, as Trump calls for Iran to accept a deal
dailyNews.description: While President Trump threatens to bomb Iran "back to the stone ages" if they do not agree to a deal to end the war, some experts tell CBS News that the continuing war will likely make the regime in Tehran more determined to build a nuclear weapon.

dailyNews.title: Missing U.S. crew member from downed fighter jet rescued in Iran, Trump says
dailyNews.description: A U.S. crew member who went missing when an F-15E fighter jet was shot down over a remote area of Iran has been rescued by U.S. forces.

dailyNews.title: Trump says rescued U.S. airman 'seriously wounded,' threatens to strike Iran power plants and bridges
dailyNews.description: Trump says the officer was rescued "deep in Enemy Territory" in mountainous terrain.

dailyNews.title: Warsh nomination moves ahead, putting Trump's competing Fed plans on a collision course
dailyNews.description: A Senate committee will consider Kevin Warsh's nomination even as one of its members still plans to block it.

dailyNews.title: Trump tariff fallout: Some industries grapple with lingering effects one year later
dailyNews.description: A year after his "liberation day," Trump's trade war has reshaped how companies in industries such as retail and autos are modeling economic and policy risk.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.

dailyNews.title: Beijing Signals Readiness to Talk to Trump's Team, Even Old Foes
dailyNews.description: The Chinese foreign minister spoke by phone with Marco Rubio, the new secretary of state who has been a critic of Beijing.

dailyNews.title: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
dailyNews.description: The drug is increasingly manufactured in and trafficked from the U.S.’s northern neighbor, though the numbers remain small compared with Mexico.
